dossier.disagreement_resolution: Research and Operator favor shipping an internal engine now; Skeptic and Risk warn against overconfidence in mock output. Chair weights execution learning over polish, but requires measurable kill criteria before expanding scope.
dossier.strongest_argument_for: Internal tooling de-risks architecture and produces reusable decision artifacts.
dossier.strongest_argument_against: Mock-only reasoning can look rigorous while remaining generic on novel questions.
dossier.deciding_factor: The cost of a small prototype is lower than committing to a full platform prematurely.
dossier.confidence_rationale: Confidence is 0.72 because specialist briefs align on sequencing (internal first) but disagree on output quality until real providers are wired.
assumptions: The team can iterate quickly without external customer commitments.
Structured dossiers will be reviewed by humans before driving major commitments.
Provider contracts remain stable as OpenAI and later vendors are added.
arguments_for: Internal tooling de-risks architecture and produces reusable decision artifacts.
Agent roles map cleanly to executive decision workflows.
Saved JSON/Markdown runs create an audit trail for revisits.
arguments_against: Mock-only reasoning can look rigorous while remaining generic on novel questions.
Manual CLI usage limits adoption until later slice enhancements.
Single-pass council may miss issues that debate rounds would surface.
risks: Scope creep into SaaS, auth, or trading features before core engine is stable.
Overfitting prompts to sample questions instead of general decision quality.
False confidence if chair synthesis reads more decisive than evidence supports.
dossier.recommendation: Proceed with a time-boxed internal prototype before external packaging.
kill_criteria: Two consecutive runs fail to produce schema-valid dossiers on representative questions.
Internal stakeholders stop referencing run artifacts within one sprint.
OpenAI mode dossiers score worse than mock baselines on a simple quality rubric.
next_actions: Run three real product decisions through mock and OpenAI modes and compare dossiers.
Add debate rounds only after baseline dossier quality is stable.
Track disagreement_resolution usefulness with a short reviewer checklist.
open_questions: Which decisions require multi-round debate versus a single council pass?
What retention policy should apply to runs stored on disk?
When should confidence scores block automated downstream actions?

agent_briefs.headline: Decision is an internal capability bet, not a go-to-market launch.
agent_briefs.role_specific_finding: The question 'Should I build this?' is primarily about build-vs-buy sequencing for an internal decision system.
agent_briefs.evidence_basis: Wording emphasizes internal tool-first delivery and engineering leverage.
agent_briefs.uncertainty: Exact stakeholder map and success metrics are not fully specified.
agent_briefs.decision_implication: Chair should optimize for learning speed and artifact quality, not distribution.
agent_briefs.reasoning: Stakeholders are likely product and engineering leaders evaluating whether to invest in an internal council workflow before external packaging.

agent_briefs.headline: Precedent supports CLI/internal tooling before UI and SaaS packaging.
agent_briefs.role_specific_finding: Comparable decision systems begin as internal workflows with saved artifacts.
agent_briefs.evidence_basis: Pattern from internal AI tooling: notebook/CLI prototypes precede productized surfaces.
agent_briefs.uncertainty: Limited evidence on how quickly mock output transfers to real LLM quality.
agent_briefs.decision_implication: Favor an internal engine milestone with explicit quality gates.
agent_briefs.reasoning: Research angle on 'Should I build this?' suggests reducing scope to structured runs that teams can review asynchronously.

agent_briefs.headline: A polished dossier can mask weak underlying reasoning in mock mode.
agent_briefs.role_specific_finding: The council may appear decisive without adversarial depth.
agent_briefs.evidence_basis: Mock agents use templated phrasing that may not stress-test the specific question.
agent_briefs.uncertainty: How much skepticism is enough before delaying a useful internal prototype.
agent_briefs.decision_implication: Chair must include kill criteria and explicit uncertainty in the final call.
agent_briefs.reasoning: For 'Should I build this?', the risk is approving internal tooling because the format looks executive-ready, not because reasoning was truly tested.

agent_briefs.headline: Scope drift and false confidence are the dominant failure modes.
agent_briefs.role_specific_finding: Adding platform features early could collapse focus on dossier quality.
agent_briefs.evidence_basis: Roadmap slices explicitly defer UI, auth, and multi-provider complexity.
agent_briefs.uncertainty: Whether kill criteria will be enforced in practice by reviewers.
agent_briefs.decision_implication: Proceed only with constraints that cap scope and require human review.
agent_briefs.reasoning: Operational risk is not building the wrong tool—it is building too much too soon while trusting low-cost mock runs as if they were audited analysis.

agent_briefs.headline: Next two weeks: harden prompts, run real decisions, compare mock vs OpenAI.
agent_briefs.role_specific_finding: Execution path is CLI-first with saved runs under versioned schema.
agent_briefs.evidence_basis: Current repo supports uv workflows, structured JSON, and provider switching via env.
agent_briefs.uncertainty: Engineering time to tune OpenAI prompts to beat mock usefulness.
agent_briefs.decision_implication: Approve internal rollout to a small pilot group with weekly dossier review.
agent_briefs.reasoning: Operators can ship value by running the council on live decisions and iterating prompts before any UI or external user onboarding.
